meta.scene_summary: We are busy building the future with microscopic chips and magic batteries while the rest of the industry tries to duct-tape the past to the EU's penalty box.

cooking.headline: OpenAI is staggering GPT-5.6 because the government wants to avoid a collective panic attack
cooking.summary: The government is treating AI releases like a regulated medicine rather than a software update. It turns out that when your model is smart enough to be dangerous, 'move fast and break things' is no longer a viable company motto.
cooking.plain_english: The United States government has requested that OpenAI release its new artificial intelligence model in stages to ensure safety and security.

cooking.headline: IBM just made a chip so small you'll need a microscope to find your bugs
cooking.summary: IBM managed to shrink transistors below one nanometer, effectively turning silicon into a literal subatomic group project. We're getting closer to the day where your CPU has more in common with a quantum physics experiment than a calculator.
cooking.plain_english: IBM has developed a new manufacturing process for computer chips that allows transistors to be smaller than one nanometer in size.

cooking.headline: Micron locked in high prices for five years, so say goodbye to your hardware budget
cooking.summary: Micron just signed a five-year contract to keep memory prices at historic highs, effectively turning RAM into a luxury subscription service. It turns out the only thing more volatile than the stock market is the cost of upgrading your workstation.
cooking.plain_english: Micron has secured long-term agreements that will keep memory chip prices at high levels for the next five years.

cooking.headline: AMD is finally letting Linux have the HDMI 2.1 keys
cooking.summary: After years of fighting over proprietary specs, AMD is finally opening up the HDMI 2.1 stack for Linux users. It's nice to see hardware manufacturers realize that if you want people to actually use your stuff, you should probably let the drivers work.
cooking.plain_english: AMD is releasing the open-source code required to fully support high-speed HDMI 2.1 display connections on Linux operating systems.

cooking.headline: The entire internet is held together by duct tape and good intentions
cooking.summary: We all rely on open source code maintained by people who are probably just one bad day away from deleting their entire GitHub repository. It turns out the digital equivalent of a group project is mostly just everyone hoping someone else remembers to pay the server bill.
cooking.plain_english: A group of organizations has formed to provide better support and security for open source software projects.

cooking.headline: Scientists just found the 'undo' button for dead batteries
cooking.summary: Researchers discovered an electrochemical bath that restores lithium batteries to 95% capacity, effectively giving your phone a second life. It's great news for the planet, though terrible news for the manufacturers who were really hoping you'd buy a new device every two years.
cooking.plain_english: A new method using an electrochemical bath has been developed to restore the capacity of degraded lithium-ion batteries.

cooked.headline: The EU is officially putting Amazon and Microsoft in the digital penalty box
cooked.summary: Europe has decided that being a massive cloud provider now comes with the same level of oversight as a public utility. It is essentially the corporate equivalent of being told you have to invite everyone in the class to your birthday party.
cooked.plain_english: The European Commission is designating Amazon and Microsoft as 'gatekeepers,' which subjects them to stricter regulations regarding fair competition in the cloud computing market.

cooked.headline: Apple is speedrunning the M-series to chase the AI hype train
cooked.summary: Apple is skipping the M6 generation entirely to push out M7 chips, proving that product roadmaps are just suggestions when there's an AI buzzword on the line. It's like throwing away your perfectly good textbook because the new edition has a different color cover.
cooked.plain_english: Apple is reportedly canceling the M6 series of computer processors to release a new M7 line specifically designed for artificial intelligence tasks.

cooked.headline: Windows 10 gets an extra year of life because nobody wants to upgrade
cooked.summary: Microsoft is begrudgingly extending Windows 10 support for another year, likely because the world is collectively refusing to move to Windows 11. It's the digital equivalent of a landlord letting you stay an extra year because they know you'll just move into a worse apartment.
cooked.plain_english: Microsoft has extended the official support period for the Windows 10 operating system by one additional year.

cooked.headline: Hasbro wants to turn child actors into infinite AI content farms
cooked.summary: Hasbro is asking child voice actors to sign away their vocal rights so AI can keep the performance going long after the kid grows up. It turns out the only thing worse than a group project partner who does no work is one that tries to replace you with a prompt.
cooked.plain_english: Hasbro is including clauses in contracts for child actors that grant the company the right to use their voices to train artificial intelligence models.
